deep_dive.title: Deep learning-based prediction of time-resolved adhesive forces in viscoelastic Hertzian contacts
deep_dive.abstract: Fast prediction of the response of adhesive soft viscoelastic contacts represents a current challenge in soft robotics and for gripping and manipulation tasks. Determining the complete time-resolved force trajectory requires full numerical simulations, whose computational cost is strongly parameter-dependent, making them impractical for real-time application or design-optimization loops. In this work, we overcome this limitation by training a scalar-conditioned, stateful, sequence-to-sequence deep learning model to predict the full force evolution from a prescribed displacement history for both short- and long-range adhesion regimes. The data set spans four orders of magnitude in loading and unloading rates and includes varied dwell times, with the Tabor parameter ranging from $0.2$ to $3.2$. To enable learning across these heterogeneous time scales, we introduce a fixed-measurement-step (FMS) representation that converts variable-length trajectories into fixed-length sequences while preserving their physical-time information. Different architectures were trained, including long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, temporal convolutional neural (TCN) networks, and time-distributed dense layers with three different Tabor-conditioning mechanisms. The models were compared using global waveform and error metrics. We found that the best-performing model has an LSTM architecture with concatenated conditioning, which achieves a held-out mean-squared error of $5.0\times10^{-4}$, a median pull-off-force error of $\approx2.2\%$, and a median hysteresis error of $\approx1.1\%$. For the held-out protocols, the model predicts a complete force trajectory with a median inference time of $0.16$ s. The model is tested across unseen parameter combinations and against analytical limiting cases, providing a rapid surrogate for repeated numerical evaluations with potential use in control-oriented applications.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: deep learning for force prediction in contacts, core to tactile sensing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 深度学习预测接触力，是触觉感知的核心
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 深度学习预测接触力，是触觉感知的核心
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 深度学习预测接触力，是触觉感知的核心
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Trains a sequence-to-sequence deep learning model to predict full time-resolved adhesive force trajectories in viscoelastic contacts, enabling fast force prediction for soft robotics gripping. Relevant to tactile foundation models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文训练了一个标量条件的状态化序列到序列深度学习模型，用于预测粘弹性赫兹接触中随时间完整的粘附力轨迹。数据集涵盖四个数量级的加载速率。该模型克服了传统数值仿真计算成本高的缺点，实现了实时力预测。该工作为触觉基础模型提供了关键的接触力预测能力，可广泛用于软体机器人抓取和操作中的触觉感知。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文训练了一个标量条件的状态化序列到序列深度学习模型，用于预测粘弹性赫兹接触中随时间完整的粘附力轨迹。数据集涵盖四个数量级的加载速率。该模型克服了传统数值仿真计算成本高的缺点，实现了实时力预测。该工作为触觉基础模型提供了关键的接触力预测能力，可广泛用于软体机器人抓取和操作中的触觉感知。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: tactile foundation model for universal touch sensing

quick_skim.title: Learning Forward & Reverse Skills from a Single Unfinished Demonstration for Constrained Manipulation Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: Learning from demonstration (LfD) enables robots to learn manipulation skills directly from expert demonstrations but remains challenging for contact-rich tasks involving geometric constraints and force interaction. Existing approaches typically require multiple complete demonstrations and do not support reverse skill execution. In this paper, we present a unified one-shot framework for constrained manipulation that learns both forward and reverse execution from a single, possibly unfinished demonstration. Our method decomposes demonstrations into non-contact and contact phases, with non-contact motion encoded with dynamic movement primitives (DMP), and contact motion represented as a sequence of screw motion primitives segmented by our proposed geometry-driven twist-direction segmentation algorithm. During execution, screw primitives are executed sequentially under admittance-guided pose correction and speed regulation, enabling task completion beyond the demonstrated trajectory length as well as reverse skill execution without additional learning data. Experiments on peg insertion, battery insertion, lock opening, and screw driving tasks demonstrate improved success rates and robustness over segmentation and one-shot trajectory learning baselines. Details are available on the project website: https://tuwien-asl.github.io/LfD-Screw/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learning manipulation with contact/force, directly relevant to tactile feedback
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从单次示范学习接触力相关操作，与触觉反馈直接相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从单次示范学习接触力相关操作，与触觉反馈直接相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从单次示范学习接触力相关操作，与触觉反馈直接相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a one-shot framework for constrained manipulation learning forward and reverse skills from a single unfinished demo, handling contact phases and force interaction. Directly relevant to tactile feedback.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一个统一框架，从单个可能未完成的示范中学习约束操作的前向和反向执行技能。方法将示范分解为非接触和接触阶段，接触阶段使用螺旋运动基元序列表示力交互。实验表明该方法能有效学习接触丰富的任务，并支持反向执行，大大降低了示范学习成本，对触觉反馈下的机器人技能学习具有重要价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一个统一框架，从单个可能未完成的示范中学习约束操作的前向和反向执行技能。方法将示范分解为非接触和接触阶段，接触阶段使用螺旋运动基元序列表示力交互。实验表明该方法能有效学习接触丰富的任务，并支持反向执行，大大降低了示范学习成本，对触觉反馈下的机器人技能学习具有重要价值。

quick_skim.title: Foresight Residual RL for Long-Horizon Robot Manipulation with Vision-Language-Action Models
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies offer strong general-purpose manipulation priors, but often fail on tight-tolerance, contact-rich assembly due to long-horizon credit assignment and subtask coupling: a state that is geometrically successful for the current skill can be brittle for downstream skills. We show this failure mode in residual reinforcement learning (RL) over a frozen VLA base policy: constant sparse success rewards improve each subtask in isolation yet yield little or no gain when skills are chained, because terminal state quality is uncontrolled. We propose Foresight Residual RL, which optimizes handoff quality by augmenting each subtask's sparse success reward with an offline-estimated foresight value -- the probability of future subtask success conditioned on the terminal state of the current subtask. Concretely, we (i) train a visual foresight predictor from images of terminal states of the base policy, labeled using downstream rollout statistics, and (ii) train residual policies via backward foresight induction, using the predictor output as a reward multiplier. On a three-phase wrench-based nut-tightening assembly task in Isaac Gym (grasp, move-insert, rotate), our method achieves 85.6% full-task success, outperforming standard subtask residual RL (54.5%) and VLA baselines, while leaving per-subtask success unchanged. These results highlight that improving long-horizon performance requires shaping which successful states are produced at each sub-task, not only whether success occurs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: VLA model with residual RL for contact-rich manipulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于接触密集型操作的VLA模型与残差强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于接触密集型操作的VLA模型与残差强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于接触密集型操作的VLA模型与残差强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Foresight Residual RL that augments VLA policies with offline-estimated foresight values to improve handoff quality in long-horizon contact-rich assembly tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）策略在接触密集型长时域操作中因子任务耦合而失败。本文提出Foresight残差强化学习，在冻结VLA基策略上为每个子任务添加稀疏成功奖励和离线估计的前瞻价值，优化子任务间状态质量。在装配任务中显著提升成功率，为接触丰富的机器人控制提供了有效方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）策略在接触密集型长时域操作中因子任务耦合而失败。本文提出Foresight残差强化学习，在冻结VLA基策略上为每个子任务添加稀疏成功奖励和离线估计的前瞻价值，优化子任务间状态质量。在装配任务中显著提升成功率，为接触丰富的机器人控制提供了有效方法。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: tactile vision language action model for robot control

quick_skim.title: HCPG-Flow:Hierarchical Contact-Progress Guidance for Flow-Policy Robot Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Flow policies can represent multimodal action distributions for robot manipulation, yet a robot must execute one action at each control step. When several proposals are sampled, critic-based ranking makes data collection depend on value estimates over candidate actions that may be weakly represented in replay. We introduce HCPG-Flow, an analytic rollout-time selector that augments SAC-Flow with hierarchical, object-centric contact-progress guidance while preserving its actor and critic objectives. HCPG switches from end-effector approach to task progress after contact, scores each proposal by the first-order reduction of a task-relevant distance, standardizes scores within the candidate set, and executes a temperature-controlled action embedding. Across ten simulated tasks, HCPG improves mean success over SAC-Flow on both benchmarks, including a 9.5 percentage-point gain on Maniskill. Four physical tasks further show high success with a 17.4% reduction in successful completion steps.Project page: https://hitxraz.github.io/HCPG-Flow/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: contact-progress guidance for robot manipulation with tactile feedback
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 接触进度引导用于机器人操作触觉反馈
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 接触进度引导用于机器人操作触觉反馈
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 接触进度引导用于机器人操作触觉反馈
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces hierarchical contact-progress guidance for flow-policy robot manipulation, using analytic rollout-time selection to improve action ranking and task performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 流策略机器人操作中，采样多种动作时基于评论家的排序可能不可靠。本文提出HCPG-Flow，在SAC-Flow基础上引入分层、以物体为中心接触进度引导：接触前采用末端执行器接近，接触后转为任务进度。通过第一性任务距离减少评分，标准化后温度控制选择动作。实验证明该方法显著提升了操作成功率和效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 流策略机器人操作中，采样多种动作时基于评论家的排序可能不可靠。本文提出HCPG-Flow，在SAC-Flow基础上引入分层、以物体为中心接触进度引导：接触前采用末端执行器接近，接触后转为任务进度。通过第一性任务距离减少评分，标准化后温度控制选择动作。实验证明该方法显著提升了操作成功率和效率。

quick_skim.title: Optimization of sim-to-real transfer in the humanoid robot NICO
quick_skim.abstract: Robotic grasping requires accurate coordination between visual perception, object localization, inverse kinematics, and hand control. However, when movements planned in simulation are executed on a physical robot, the sim-to-real gap can cause small positioning errors that prevent successful grasping. In our previous work, we introduced a low-cost haptic calibration method that improved 2D reaching accuracy of the humanoid robot NICO. In this paper, we extend this approach from reaching to tabletop object grasping by adding YOLO-based object and hand detection, stereo vision-based localization using the robot's built-in low-resolution fisheye cameras, and task-specific corrections for grasp execution. Together, these components form a novel calibration-based grasping pipeline that does not require RGB-D cameras, motion capture, or external tracking systems. We also implemented a visual feedback model that aligns the robot hand with the detected object before grasping. Our results show that the fully nonlinear calibration model achieved the best performance inside the calibrated area, while the visual feedback model achieved the highest overall grasping success across the full tabletop workspace.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: haptic calibration for sim-to-real transfer in grasping
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 抓取中利用触觉校准的仿真到现实迁移
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 抓取中利用触觉校准的仿真到现实迁移
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 抓取中利用触觉校准的仿真到现实迁移
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extends a low-cost haptic calibration method to improve tabletop grasping accuracy on the NICO humanoid robot, using YOLO detection and stereo vision. Demonstrates robot learning with tactile feedback.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 仿真到现实迁移中的微小定位误差阻碍机器人抓取。本文在原有触觉校准方法基础上，增加YOLO检测和立体视觉定位，优化抓取执行。通过触觉反馈修正仿真策略，提升了真实世界抓取成功率，展示了触觉反馈在机器人学习中的价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 仿真到现实迁移中的微小定位误差阻碍机器人抓取。本文在原有触觉校准方法基础上，增加YOLO检测和立体视觉定位，优化抓取执行。通过触觉反馈修正仿真策略，提升了真实世界抓取成功率，展示了触觉反馈在机器人学习中的价值。

quick_skim.title: ModPack: An Extensible Teleoperation Interface for Bimanual Mobile Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Existing teleoperation systems are often tailored to specific robot hardware and task domains, limiting their scalability and adaptability. We present ModPack, a modular and extensible teleoperation system designed to support diverse robot embodiments and task requirements within a unified framework. At the core of ModPack is a self-contained wearable "backpack" that integrates onboard computation, power, communication, and data storage. Built on top of this shared interface, the system supports plug-and-play capability modules including joint-level teleoperation with haptic feedback, mobile manipulation, and active perception. Experiments across two distinct robot platforms and real-world mobile manipulation tasks demonstrate that ModPack provides a flexible and reusable framework for data collection and policy learning. To support future research, we open-source the complete hardware design and software stack. Project website: https://modpack-robotics.github.io/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: teleoperation system with haptic feedback for robot learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带有触觉反馈的遥操作系统用于机器人学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带有触觉反馈的遥操作系统用于机器人学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带有触觉反馈的遥操作系统用于机器人学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ModPack provides a modular teleoperation interface with haptic feedback for bimanual mobile manipulation, supporting plug-and-play capability modules.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有遥操作系统专用性强、扩展性差的问题，论文提出ModPack模块化遥操作接口，核心为可穿戴背包集成计算、供电与通信。系统支持插拔式模块，包括带触觉反馈的关节级遥操作、移动操作和主动感知。在两个不同机器人平台上的实验表明，该系统在多种移动操作任务中表现高效，为机器人学习提供了可复用的触觉反馈平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有遥操作系统专用性强、扩展性差的问题，论文提出ModPack模块化遥操作接口，核心为可穿戴背包集成计算、供电与通信。系统支持插拔式模块，包括带触觉反馈的关节级遥操作、移动操作和主动感知。在两个不同机器人平台上的实验表明，该系统在多种移动操作任务中表现高效，为机器人学习提供了可复用的触觉反馈平台。
